reasoning: The automated programmatic checks identified a critical pipeline failure where header/footer elements were pervasively retained across the document, which causes screen readers to encounter duplicate artifacts on every page. Additionally, there are multiple major defects including displaced hyperlinks, improper table header markup, and footnotes improperly converted to lists. Due to the presence of a critical programmatic defect, the document must be rejected.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (8): Wetlands, Buffers, Stormwater - Compliance and Permitting Un; An Equal Opportunity \ Affirmative Action Employer; Page 2 of 7
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=8, pages_affected=7/7. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 7 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=7, examples=['http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wq/swp/ws/401/publicnotices', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wq/swp/ws/401/certsandpermits/m', 'mailto:katie.merritt@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "Please note that the US Army Corps of Engineers is". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Corps’ Regional General Permit"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

action_items: Adjust pipeline settings to exclude recurring headers and footers.
Ensure hyperlinks are rendered inline within their containing paragraphs.
Correct table header markup and preserve footnote semantics.
